body:
Surfaced by the post-#292 exhaustiveness sweep — the read-side twin of #346. It is **not** an interpretation of `ChunkRef.placement` (so #292's "audit every path that interprets placement" framing did not catch it); it is a place where the already-**resolved** placement is dropped at the trait boundary.

## Problem

The read path resolves each fragment to its placed D-server via `ChunkRef::placed_dserver` and calls `PlacementChunkStore::get_fragment_at(dserver, id)` (`crates/core/src/read.rs:127`). But `FanoutChunkStore` implements `PlacementChunkStore` with the **default** `get_fragment_at` (`crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/fanout.rs:120` — empty impl; default in `crates/traits/src/lib.rs`), which **ignores `dserver`** and delegates to `get_fragment(id)` → `route(id.index)` = `stores[index % n]`. So the fan-out routes by **fragment index**, never by the **placed D-server**.

That is correct only while placement is identity. Once a custodian **moves** a fragment — rebalance evacuation or reconstruction re-placement repoints `placement[i]` to a new D-server — a read through the fan-out fetches that fragment from its **old** store:

- `EcScheme::None`: the single fragment → total miss → the object becomes unreadable.
- `ReedSolomon`: insidious — the read "reads around" the wrong-located fragment and still succeeds, **silently masking** the gap while eroding the redundancy margin; enough moves drop below `k`.

The code acknowledges and defers exactly this — `fanout.rs:18-19`: *"The fan-out is no longer the location authority … Honouring a moved id (a relocatable, custodian-aware fan-out) is a later M3 slice."* This issue is that slice. Production multi-D-server deployments that read through an id-indexed fleet of `GrpcChunkStore`s already honor moved placement (e.g. the reconstruction/rebalance tests); the exposure is the fan-out path — the CLI `cluster_store_get` / `GrpcFanout` and single-machine clusters (#155) — once custodians move fragments.

## Fix

Override `FanoutChunkStore::get_fragment_at` (and `put_fragment_at`) to route by the **placed D-server id** the read path resolves (`stores[dserver % n]`, or a proper D-server-id → store map) instead of the default index-based routing. Then a moved fragment is fetched from the store the placement record names, matching the id-indexed fleet path.

## Test

A read of a chunk whose committed `placement` repoints fragment `i` to a non-identity D-server, served through a `FanoutChunkStore`, must return the moved fragment — fails today (routed to `stores[i % n]`, the old store), passes after. Mirror `core/tests/placement_record.rs::moved_fragment_resolved_from_record_after_reopen`, but over the fan-out rather than the id-indexed fleet. Cover `EcScheme::None` (total miss) and a Reed-Solomon move.

Refs #292 (the audit that surfaced it) · #346 (sibling — the write-side twin) · #139 (the placement record).

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Relocatable / custodian-aware fan-out: FanoutChunkStore::get_fragment_at must route by the placed D-server, not index % n
